action.reasoning: Observation: 4-model ConvNeXt ensemble got 0.7975, slightly worse than 3-model ensemble at 0.7998. cnxt4 had lower val_acc (0.5770) and hurt the ensemble. Analysis: Need to remove cnxt4 from ensemble, and try a different diversification - perhaps a model with stronger recipe (more epochs, slightly different) that achieves higher individual quality. With 12 iterations and 4.45h left, I can: (1) revert to 3-model ensemble (best=0.7998), (2) train cnxt5 with better recipe and replace cnxt4. Decision: First, just revert by removing cnxt4 from predict.py and confirm 0.7998. Then train one more strong model with longer training (6 epochs, careful LR) and see if it helps.
